dataset_manifest.quality_notes: Synthetic data lacks real-world artifacts; real datasets must be inspected per the quality_checks item above.
dataset_manifest.sample_description: Synthetic dataset with 40 excitatory and 10 inhibitory units, 60 s duration, sampling 1 kHz.

figures.caption: Illustrative synthetic example: top panel shows LFP-like signal with perturbation epoch shaded in orange; center shows a raster of simulated excitatory (blue) and inhibitory (orange) spikes (inhibitory spikes are reduced during the perturbation); bottom shows smoothed population rate. Right-hand panel compares pairwise correlation distributions pre and during perturbation. Produced by analysis.py in demo mode.

findings.claim: In a synthetic demonstration dataset, acute reduction of inhibitory firing is associated with a modest increase in pairwise spike-time correlations among excitatory neurons.
findings.estimate: Median pairwise Pearson correlation changed from approximately -0.0041 (pre) to -0.0028 (during perturbation) for 50-ms binned counts in the synthetic run.
findings.evidence: Synthetic spike trains and LFP were generated with increased shared event probability during the perturbation epoch; pairwise correlations were computed across time bins. Figure_1 shows the raster, LFP, population rate, and correlation histograms.
findings.uncertainty: These values are from a single synthetic simulation and should not be interpreted as empirical estimates. Real data will show session-level variability and require bootstrapping/mixed-effects modeling for confidence intervals.

limitations: No real public NWB dataset was downloaded or analyzed in this execution environment; included results are from a synthetic demonstration and do not constitute empirical evidence.
Unit classification using extracellular waveform features is imperfect and requires validation against anatomical/optogenetic tagging when possible.
Optogenetic artifacts can bias both spike detection and LFP; artifact detection/removal is necessary when analyzing real datasets.
Observed correlation changes can be driven by firing-rate changes; surrogate controls (jitter, shift predictors) are necessary to dissociate rate effects.
narrative_summary: The provided pipeline demonstrates how to quantify changes in coupling and synchrony following interneuron dampening. In the synthetic example, inhibitory firing was strongly reduced during a 20-40 s perturbation, which produced a modest increase in measured pairwise correlations among excitatory neurons using 50 ms binned spike counts. Population rate and raster plots illustrate increased shared events during perturbation. The bundle provides code and scaffolding necessary to apply the same analyses to real NWB datasets.

provenance.notes: This analysis demonstrates code and expected outputs. To perform empirical analysis, run analysis.py in an environment with network access and point it to appropriate NWB/DANDI datasets.

tables.notes: Numbers are from the illustrative synthetic run and are included to show example outputs of analysis.py.
tables.title: Synthetic dataset summary statistics
